description: Core business logic imports a concrete vendor or I/O client directly, so every infrastructure change ripples into the policy layer.
body: Failure mode: high-level policy code directly imports a specific database driver, HTTP client, or vendor SDK, so swapping or upgrading the detail forces edits to core logic and makes the policy untestable without that system. Detection signals: business modules importing concrete clients; tests requiring real infrastructure; churn in core logic driven by infra changes. Do not let stable policy depend on volatile detail. Safer fallback: define an interface owned by the policy layer, have the concrete adapter implement it, inject it, and verify the dependency now points from detail to policy.
